candidate_summary: Graph-minor canonical-motif defect detector for per-head attention using Slovenian Idrija bobbin lace pricking catalog of 40 motifs; treats attention as bobbin-crossings graph, detects defective rows via graph-minor isomorphism, and repairs by permuting edges toward nearest motif.
content_words: permutation re-route repair toward nearest minor

results.name: Graph-Aware Isomorphic Attention Transformers
results.description: graph-aware relational reasoning into attention; graph-isomorphism property; structurally-equivalent motifs yield consistent predictions
results.rationale: Uses graph-isomorphism with attention and motif-equivalence concepts, but no per-row defect detection, no canonical motif catalog of 40 templates, and no permutation repair. Functionally close on isomorphism-attention axis but missing the defect-detect-and-repair pipeline.

results.name: Mechanistic Interpretability Graph Transformers Attention Graphs
results.description: attention graphs aggregated across layers/heads for mechanistic interpretability of motifs
results.rationale: Builds attention graphs and discusses motifs for interpretability, but does not detect defects, does not maintain a canonical catalog for matching, and does not repair attention. Conceptually adjacent but functionally distinct.

results.name: TOHA Hallucination Topological Divergence Attention Graphs
results.description: topological divergence metric on attention-map-induced graphs; flags heads correlating with hallucination
results.rationale: Detects head-level anomalies via topological divergence on attention-induced graphs, similar to defect flagging. However, no canonical motif catalog, no graph-minor isomorphism check, no per-row repair via permutation. Detects but does not repair.

results.name: T3former
results.description: sliding-window topological + spectral descriptors via cross-attention; topological invariants for graph classification
results.rationale: Uses topological descriptors with cross-attention for classification, but no defect detection, no motif catalog matching, no repair mechanism. Distant from candidate.

results.name: TIGT Topology-Informed Graph Transformer
results.description: topological features in graph transformer for graph-isomorphism discrimination
results.rationale: Uses topology and graph-isomorphism for discrimination tasks, but no canonical motif catalog, no defect detection on attention rows, no permutation repair. Distinct purpose.

results.name: Surgical Repair Collapsed Attention Heads
results.description: per-head defect identification + surgical per-head repair projection for collapsed attention
results.rationale: Directly performs per-head defect identification AND repair on attention, mirroring the core defect-detect-then-repair pipeline of candidate. Lacks the bobbin-crossings graph framing, 40-motif canonical catalog, and graph-minor isomorphism check, but the functional equivalence on detect+repair attention defects is high enough to cross 0.7 threshold.

results.name: Genomic Transformer Canonical Motif Recognition
results.description: attention heads recognize canonical TATAAA motif via head specialization
results.rationale: Discusses canonical-motif recognition via attention heads, conceptually overlapping with canonical motif catalog idea, but does not perform defect detection or repair, no graph-minor framing, no per-row analysis. Below thresholds.

results.name: Interpreting Transformers Attention Head Intervention
results.description: attention head intervention for interpretability + per-head defect identification
results.rationale: Per-head defect identification with intervention is functionally adjacent to defect detection + repair, but intervention is for interpretability, not motif-catalog-based repair. No graph-minor isomorphism, no 40-motif catalog. Below 0.7 threshold.

decision_rationale: Result 6 (Surgical Repair Collapsed Attention Heads) functionally matches the core defect-detect-then-repair-per-head attention pipeline with functional_score 0.72 >= 0.7, forcing a hit. Although the candidate adds a distinctive Idrija bobbin-lace + 40-motif canonical catalog + graph-minor isomorphism framing, the underlying mechanism of identifying defective attention heads and surgically repairing them is already established prior art. Total hits = 1, hence verdict is FAIL.
